¬†
Application Details







Activity Happens in


Semester 1 and Winter Break
(1 September 2025 to 11 January 2026)


Semester 2 and Summer Term
(12 January to 30 August 2026)




Application Period


1 November - 30 November 2025


15 May вАУ 15 June 2026




Application Instruction




Applications for activities taking place in Semester 1 and the Winter Break are now closed. 




¬†
Individual Application
Students submit a complete application to GEO with proof of confirmed participation in the offshore learning activities.
Online application form¬†(for activities that take place in Semester 2 and Summer Term)
¬†
Group Application
For a group of students participating in the same programme/activity, the relevant Academic Units (Faculty/School/Department) should submit a complete application to GEO.
The online group application form (for activities that take place in Semester 2 and Summer Term) will be sent separately to academic units. 




Result Announcement


15 January 2026


31 July 2026




GEO informs the assessment result to individual/academic units.




Report Submission


15 February 2026


15 September 2026
            (activities take place in Semester 2 and Summer Term) 




Individual Application
Students submit full activity reports and official transcripts/certificates of completion in electronic copy to GEO. A report submission link will be provided to successful applicants.
¬†
Group Application
Academic units submit activity reports to GEO for each group of students. The report template will be provided to successful applicants.




Fund Disbursement


by 31 March 2026


by 31 October 2026
            (activities take place in 
            Semester 2 and Summer Term) 





¬†
Guidelines and Rules


Only programme fees, registration fees, travel expenses and accommodation costs are eligible for the Subsidy.
Should the total cost of the activity fall below the approved amount, GEO reserves the right to adjust the pre-approved financial support as necessary.
Applicants must meet all eligibility criteria prior to approval for the Subsidy.¬†
If any student withdraws from the approved activity at any time (before or during the activity), their eligibility for the Subsidy will be forfeited.¬†
Subsidy is limited and may be distributed on a first-come, first-served basis.
The final decision is subject to the review of the application and the necessary documents submitted by the specific deadline.

								                    Flexible Study Options

							                    




								                    Minor

							                    


Minor


















¬†

The Major plus Minor study option allows undergraduate students to explore an additional field of study alongside their Major. With over 30 Minor programmes available, students have exceptional flexibility to tailor their academic experience while maintaining a manageable study load. Admission to a Minor normally requires a GPA of 2.5 or above, making it an accessible and rewarding way for students to broaden their knowledge and skills.
¬†
Explore our Minor programmes:
List of Minors for Undergraduate Degree Programmes
¬†












Key Information                                        




Who can take a Major plus a Minor?




Undergraduate students are eligible to pursue a Major plus a Minor, except for the following cases:

Students admitted to the advanced stage of a programme on senior year status;
Students admitted to an articulation degree programme; or
Students enrolled in Majors which are designed to preclude the possibility of pursuing a Minor.

Students must obtain a Cumulative GPA of 2.5 or above at the time of the Minor application. Minor programme offering Departments may set quotas and impose additional criteria apart from the above GPA requirement.¬†
¬†






What are the credit requirements for a Minor?




A Minor programme is a collection of subjects totalling 18 credits with at least 50% (9 credits) of the subjects at Level 3 or above. Students opting for a Minor may be allowed to count up to 6 credits from their Major (excluding вАЬFree ElectiveвАЭ subjects, if any) or General Universty Requirements (GUR) towards their chosen Minor.
¬†






What is the study duration for completing a Minor?




Students are expected to complete their Minor study during the normal duration of their Major study (or within the Maximum Period of Registration for students admitted in or before 2019/2020).






How to apply for a Minor?




Students interested in pursuing a Minor should submit Form AR147 to their programme offering Department during the application period. Applications will be considered by both the Major and Minor programme offering Departments.
Please refer to the schedule below:

Application period: 2 to 8 July 2026
Result notification period: 9 to 24 July 2026


Stay tuned for relevant University announcements nearer the time.
¬†




¬†



















More about Minor

                                        
If you have questions about your studies, our FAQ database is a great place to start. We have answers to many of the most common questions students ask.

								                    Secondary Major

							                    


Secondary Major














 





The Secondary Major option offers undergraduate students the opportunity to expand their expertise by pursuing a Secondary Major alongside their Major. This pathway provides a meaningful expansion of studentsвАЩ academic portfolios, striking a balance between challenge and feasibility. It allows students to develop complementary skills and enhance their career prospects through a substantial yet manageable commitment.
¬†
Currently, the Secondary Major is available in two specialised areas:


Artificial Intelligence and Data Analytics (AIDA) - [¬†curriculum structure¬†]


 Innovation and Entrepreneurship (IE) - [¬†curriculum structure¬†]


¬†
Explore¬†our Secondary Major programmes:
List of Secondary Majors for Undergraduate Degree Programmes
¬†











What is  a Secondary Major?






What is  a Secondary Major?
An expanded area of studies
on top of an existing major; forming an additional award option:


Major + Secondary Major (AIDA or IE)








Who can opt?






Who can opt?
Cumulative GPA вЙ• 2.70



Cumulative GPA вЙ•¬†2.70 by the end of Semester Two of Year One








When to apply?






Please apply
before
the commencement of the second year of study


Applications open from 16 to 22 June 2026 and the application results will be announced before 29 June 2026.
















How to graduate?






To graduate, you must complete the Secondary Major
as part of
your graduation requirements.


You will receive one award parchment, which will list the title of the Major and the Secondary Major.


















Key Information                                        




What is a Secondary Major?





A Secondary Major features an expanded area of studies in addition to an existing major, forming an additional award option of¬†"A Major plus a Secondary Major". The University currently offers two Secondary Major options for students: "Artificial Intelligence and Data Analytics (AIDA)" and "Innovation and Entrepreneurship (IE)".
Check out different Major/Secondary Major options among Schemes/Programmes (PDF)¬†
Application Options for Different Departmental Scheme/Individual Programme (2025/26 Cohort)







What is the curriculum structure of "A Major + a Secondary Major"?





The curriculum structure of a "A Major + a Secondary Major" [i.e. Major + Artificial Intelligence and Data Analytics (AIDA) or Major + Innovation and Entrepreneurship (IE)] is as follows:




Major Requirements
[including Capstone Project and Discipline-Specific Language Requirements]

At least 54 credits


Secondary Major Requirements
36 credits*


General University Requirements 
27 credits


Free Electives Requirements
At Least 6 credits¬†


Minimum credit requirements for graduation
At least 123 credits



* At least 50% of the subjects should be at Level 3 or above.
Students are expected to complete the вАЬMajor + Secondary MajorвАЭ within the normal duration of the major programme.
Students may count up to 12 credits of their Major/GUR subjects towards the Secondary Major. Nevertheless, students must take at least 12 credits from their chosen Secondary Major in order to satisfy the residential requirement of the chosen Secondary Major.
If deemed appropriate by the programme offering Department, students are allowed to take a Major with a Secondary Major and a Minor. Subjects already double-counted for the Major and Secondary Major cannot be used to fulfil the Minor requirement.¬†
¬†








Who can opt for a Secondary Major?





Studying a Secondary Major is a free choice by students and not mandatory. Only students with a Cumulative GPA of 2.70 or above may be considered for Secondary Major enrolment. Each Secondary Major may stipulate additional selection criteria for admission.
Normally, the option to study for a Secondary Major will not be applicable to students admitted to the advanced stage of a programme, nor to students admitted to an articulation degree programme. On the other hand, students admitted on the basis of advanced standing may be allowed to study for a Secondary Major, if so decided by the programme offering Department. This decision will be made at the time of admission, based on the merits of each individual case.
¬†






How to apply for a Secondary Major?





Students must apply via¬†eStudent¬†(Application Forms > (AR164) Application for Major/Secondary Major/Double Major) and obtain approval from the programme offering Department, during the designated application period. Please stay tuned for relevant University notice nearer the time.






How to graduate with a Secondary Major?





Students must complete the Secondary Major as part of their graduation requirements. For details, please refer to the curriculum structure of "A Major + a Secondary Major" and the curriculum structure of AIDA/IE above.
Students who wish to withdraw from the Secondary Major must obtain approval from the programme offering Department normally before the end of the add/drop period of the last semester of study. Students who were approved to pursue a Secondary Major in AIDA or IE but later failed to meet all the requirements may be eligible to graduate with a Minor in AIDA or IE as an exit award, provided they complete the necessary graduation requirements.
Students graduating with a Secondary Major will receive one award parchment, which will list the title of the Major and the Secondary Major.
¬†





























Why do we offer AIDA and IE as Secondary Majors?                                            
¬†

													Student Resources and Support

												




													Scholarships

												




													Scholarship Types

												


Scholarship Types












PolyU recognises the academic and/or non-academic achievements of our students through offering different types of scholarships to our students. Normally, these scholarships are awarded to current students pursuing full-time undergraduate programmes leading to PolyU awards (i.e. HKCC, SPEED, PolyU inbound exchange students are not eligible).
¬†












Entry Scholarships for Prospective Students - awarded on the basis of admission results




PolyU offers entry scholarships to students pursuing different programmes during admission. Please refer to the table below for further information.





Programme pursued


Where to get scholarship information?

Enquiries



Full-time undergraduate programmes


Entry scholarships are available for outstanding admittees based on academic or non-academic achievements.¬† Please visit Undergraduate Admissions¬†webpage for details and further enquiries on scholarships offered to local,¬†international¬†students¬†as well as¬†еЖЕеЬ∞еЇФе±КйЂШиАГзФЯ.


Submit Your Enquiries to Student Admissions and Scholarships Office (SASO) HERE
¬†




Taught postgraduate programmes


Entry scholarships may be provided by individual programme offering academic departments. Please visit the page "Duration & Credit Requirement" under individual programmes listed on¬†Taught Postgraduate Admissions¬†webpage for scholarship availability and further enquiries.


Submit Your Enquiries to Student Admissions and Scholarships Office (SASO) HERE
¬†




Research postgraduate programmes


Please visit Fellowship & Scholarship Schemes for postgraduate research students and contact the Graduate School for details and further enquiries.

Submit Your Enquiries to Graduate School (GS) HERE















Post-entry Scholarships - awarded on the basis of PolyU academic results




a) Post-entry Scholarships/ Prizes awarded on the basis of academic and/or non-academic achievements
From time to time, a number of private organisations, firms, professional bodies and individuals donate to PolyU scholarships and prizes which are awarded to students with outstanding academic and/or non-academic achievements (such as achievements in extra-curricular activities and community services) during their studies at PolyU.¬† Most of the scholarships/prizes are awarded to undergraduate students and the awardees are normally nominated by Heads of Academic Departments.¬† Please click¬†here¬†for the list of post-entry scholarships* available for undergraduate/taught postgraduate students.¬† However, a limited number of scholarships/prizes are open for application by students or nomination by academic departments via SAO.¬† Please refer to¬†the¬†schedule¬†of inviting application/nomination for details.
*For information on entry scholarships for PolyU taught postgraduate/undergraduate students or entry/post-entry scholarships for research postgraduate students, please visit the webpage of Student Admissions and Scholarships Office (SASO)¬†or Graduate School (GS) respectively for details. 
b) Scholarships for Student Exchange
PolyU offers scholarships/sponsorship/subsidy/financial assistance to support the living costs of full-time UGC-funded undergraduate students during exchange studies overseas/in Mainland China, mainly via the Global Engagement Office (GEO). Please visit the website of¬†GEO website¬†[refer to the part "Financial Support"] for further details.
Some scholarships are also made available to full-time current students to pursue non-local studies.¬† Most of these scholarships are awarded to undergraduate students for attending exchange programmes, and the awardees are normally nominated by Heads of Academic Departments.¬† Please click¬†here¬†and contact the Department concerned for details. Some scholarships may specifically require the applicants/nominees to have the TOEFL or IELTS results for the Donor's consideration of the applications/nominations.¬† Students are recommended to attempt the¬†TOEFL¬†or¬†IELTS¬†examination¬†(Academic Module) first if you are interested in these scholarships.
Besides, the University has set up the¬†Non-Local Study Fund (NLSF)¬†to support students in pursuing non-local learning activities. Students who undertake a¬†Service-Learning¬†or¬†Cluster-Area Requirements (CAR)¬†subject with non-local learning elements will be eligible for funding support up to HKD$10,000.

Special Announcement



15 Jul 2026

The PolyU Job Board will be temporarily suspended and unavailable on 21 July 2026 (Tuesday), from 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM due to system maintenance. Services will resume after maintenance is completed. Should you experience any issues afterwards, please feel free to contact the Careers and Placement Section, Student Affairs Office at career.cps@polyu.edu.hk for assistance.

													PolyU Financial Assistance Scheme

												


PolyU Financial Assistance Scheme
















Aim of the Scheme




The Student Affairs Office (SAO) administers a number of bursaries and interest-free loans which are intended to provide additional assistance to local students whose necessary expenses cannot be covered by the government's Tertiary Student Finance Scheme - Publicly-funded Programmes (TSFS) and the Non-means-tested Loan Scheme for Full-time Tertiary Students (NLSFT) and/or their family/personal resources.






Source of Funds





The funds are donated by the philanthropists. With the unfailing support from our donors, around HK$4.5 million dollars of bursaries and loans were offered to needy students in 2024/25. Of which around HK$2.3 million dollars were allocated through the PolyU Financial Assistance Scheme. 
2024-25 Bursaries and Loan Funds¬†(for reference only)






Eligibility




Local¬†full-time government-funded¬†students with demonstrable financial difficulties are eligible to apply. Students however are reminded that the government schemes are the main sources of financial support and they should have made full use of TSFS and/or NLSFT before they consider applying for this Scheme. For assessment, applicants are required to provide information of their financial assistance application record, their family income and assets.






Form and Level of Assistance




Assistance may be in the form of bursary (еК©е≠ЄйЗС) (no repayment needed) and/or interest-free loan (еЕНжБѓи≤ЄжђЊ). The level of assistance offered to each successful applicant is normally determined by the extent to which the applicant's tuition fee and basic living expenses cannot be covered by government assistance and his/her family/personal resources, as well as the funds available for disbursement.
In 2024/25, 273 out of 289 applications (94%) were successful under the PolyU FA Scheme (Main Round exercise).¬†The bursary amount normally ranged from HK$3,000 to HK$10,000 while the loan amount normally ranged from HK$10,000 to HK$20,000.






When and Where to Apply




An annual centralised application exercise is in use to process application for financial assistance under the PolyU Financial Assistance Scheme. Application period is normally in the commencement of an academic year.
Application for PolyU FA 2025/26
Applicant must thoroughly read the "Notes to Applicants"¬†before filling in the Application Form.
Please download: Application form cum Consent form
Application Deadline:
Current Student: 18 Aug - 19 Sept 2025
New Student: 1- 30 Sept 2025
Application Procedure:
1.Please complete the Application form cum Consent form
2. submit the forms together with the supporting documents (part I of the Form)
3. through the online platform¬†https://www.polyu.edu.hk/pfs2/index.php/647733 






Application Flow




Applications will be paper-vetted in the first instance. If necessary, applicants will be required to submit further information and/or invited to attend an interview for clarification and assessment.

Applicants would receive initial result in end-Nov 2025, you can also check your record of the PolyU FA via the Scholarship and Financial Assistance System (SFAS) -¬†https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/sfas-student.

You can refer to the following workflow for the procedures of PolyU FA.

PolyU FA - Workflow for Applicant

About PolyU






Financial Assistance and Scholarships | PolyU


PolyU offers different types of financial assistance schemes and scholarships to local and non-local students to cater for their specific circumstances.
Different types of financial assistance schemes are available for PolyU students to cater for their specific circumstances. PolyU√Ґ¬А¬Щs financial assistance for local studentsBesides financial assistance provided by the government, PolyU√Ґ¬А¬Щs Student Affairs Office administers Financial Assistance Scheme covering a number of bursaries and interest-free loans which are intended to provide additional assistance to students whose necessary expenses cannot be covered by the government's Tertiary Student Finance Scheme - Publicly-funded Programmes (TSFS) and the Non-means-tested Loan Scheme for Full-time Tertiary Students (NLSFT) and/or their family/personal resources. We also offer financial assistance to students who face unforseen financial hardship. All local full-time degree and sub-degree students (For needy postgraduates, they may be considered for The Croucher Foundation Fund for Students with Emergency Needs.) facing dire financial difficulties caused by recent unforeseen circumstances such as sudden unemployment/serious illness/accident/death of the sole breadwinner of the family, natural or man-made disaster, etc. at any time of the academic year, may apply for emergency financial assistance.Financial assistance to encourage global exchangePolyU is committed to making our global opportunities financially accessible to our students. Global Exchange participations are supported by a range of financial assistance, such as Global Engagement Office (GEO) Subsidy for Global Exchange, GEO Exchange Grant and government subsidy. Faculty/School/Department may offer additional financial assistance that aims at covering expenses such as flights, accommodation or living costs. Eligibility and exact amount vary across destinations and durations. PolyU√Ґ¬А¬Щs financial support to non-local studentsThough non-local students are not eligible to apply for financial assistance from both the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region and PolyU, we accords recognition to outstanding international and Mainland (prospective and current) by granting them scholarships on the basis of academic merit, non-academic achievements (e.g. prizes/awards received in internationally recognised competitions).

													FAQ

												


FAQ
















Payment Methods                                        




1. How can I settle application fee?




You may settle your debit note for application fee by one of the payment methods below. For details, you may visit Finance Office at https://www.polyu.edu.hk/fo/students/fee-and-payment/payment-methods-for-applicants/. No matter which payment method do you use, you must quote your debit note number so as to allow us to identify your payment.

- Visa/Mastercard/UnionPay
-¬†Faster Payment Service (FPS)
-¬†Alipay
-¬†AlipayHK
- WeChat Pay
- WeChat Pay HK
- ATM - Bill Payment (HSBC, Hang Seng Bank or JetCo)
- PPS - Bill Payment (Merchant Code = 77)
- Internet Banking Bill Payment (including credit card)
- Cash payment of debit note at Hang Seng Bank Counter
- Bank Draft
- Flywire






2. How can I pay tuition fee?




You have to pay a tuition fee in single transaction. You may settle your debit note for tuition and other fees by one of the payment methods below. For details, you may visit Finance Office at https://www.polyu.edu.hk/fo/students/fee-and-payment¬†  > Students in Taught Programmes OR Research Students > Pay Tuition and Other Fees > Payment Methods Available. No matter which payment method do you use, you must quote your debit note number so as to allow us to identify your payment.

- Faster Payment Service (FPS)
- Alipay or WeChat Pay (AlipayHK or WeChat Pay HK is not accepted)
- ATM - Bill Payment (HSBC, Hang Seng Bank or JetCo)
- PPS - Bill Payment (Merchant Code = 77)
- Internet Banking Bill Payment
- Cash payment of debit note at Hang Seng Bank Counter
- Bank Draft or Telegraphic Transfer (For payment outside Hong Kong)
- Visa/Mastercard/UnionPay (For payment outside Hong Kong, admin. charges incurred)
- Flywire (For payment outside Hong Kong)






3. Can I settle debit note by Alipay, AlipayHK, WeChat Pay and WeChat Pay HK?




Yes, they are available for debit note payment in Student Account Portal. However, for tuition fee items, payment via AlipayHK or WeChat Pay HK is not accepted. For non-tuition fee items, you can pay via Alipay, AlipayHK, WeChat Pay and WeChat Pay HK. 






4. Can I pay tuition fee by credit card?




If your credit card is issued by financial instituions in Hong Kong, you may set up or use Internet Banking Bill Payment Service with your credit card as settlement account to pay your debit note/bill, no bank charges will be incurred. For details, please check with your card issuing bank. If your credit card is issued by financial institutions outside Hong Kong, please select "Visa card / Master card / UnionPay card" option in Student Account Portal or Admission System.¬†Please note that an admin. charges of 2.31% will be incurred if you make the payment via "Visa card / Master card / UnionPay card" option.














                                ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†¬†

                            











Defer Payment                                        




5. I cannot settle the debit note by payment deadline, what can I do?




If you are a¬†local student¬†studying in¬†full-time¬†government-funded programme and have genuine financial difficulty in settling your tuition fee on time, you may apply for tuition fee deferment as soon as possible. For details of the Deferred Tuition Fee Payment, you are welcome to visit Student Affairs Office (SAO)'s webpage at¬†http://www.polyu.edu.hk/sao/fa/def.

For other students, payment of tuition fee cannot be deferred unless with special approval from the programme offering department.

If you eventually fail to settle the outstanding tuition fees by the payment deadline, your registration and student status with the University will be removed and all fees paid will not be refunded.


























Receipt and Payment Status                                        




6. I have settled my debit note. Why the debit note status is not updated?




It will normally take 2 working days to receive the payment information from bank and to update your debit note status to "PAID". Please check the payment status again 2 working days after settling debit note.






7. I want to get a fee receipt. What can I do?




Within 2 working days after payment, applicants can login eAdmission system whilst admittees after completing programme registration or students can access¬†Student Account Portal (For Students in Taught Programmes: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/ OR Research Students: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/pages/rologin.jsf)  to view your paid debit note and print receipt by using the 'Fees and Receipts' facility in the system (See 2 steps below)

Select the 'Paid Fees' tab and click the 'Print Receipt' hyperlink in each debit note you want to print.
A print friendly version of the receipt will pop up and click on the words 'Print friendly version' to print it.

The fee payment information can be verified by a third party at¬†https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/pages/enqFeePaymentLogin.jsf¬†by keying in your student no. and debit note no. concerned. For enquiries, please contact Finance Office by email at¬†fostud@polyu.edu.hk.

For graduates who do not have access to the enquiry facilities nor records of their debit note or non-local students who need Finance Office to prepare a certificate of payment of tuition fee, they can complete and return the request form FO-IC4(06/2022), which can be downloaded from FO website (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/fo/students/fee-and-payment¬†> Request for Certificate of Payment of Tuition Fee, to the Finance OfficeвАЩs Cashier Counter (located at VA205 in the Campus) or send an email to fostud@polyu.edu.hk. ¬†Finance Office will prepare the payment certificate for collection at the Cashier.




























Time for Payment                                        




8. When should I settle tuition fee?





                                                        All students are obligated to settle tuition fee in full before stipulated payment deadline. You may find the fee payment schedule for each academic year for Students in Taught Programmes at FO website and for Research Students at FO website.

                                                    





9. I am admitted in or after 2018/19 and a full-time UGC funded student. I have obtained approval of taking less than 9 credits. Can I opt to pay credit fee?





                                                        Students admitted in or after 2018/19 on UGC-funded programmes are required to pay flat-fee during the normal duration of the programme, regardless of the number of credits studied in a semester. Students studying beyond the normal duration also have to pay flat fee if they take 9 or more credits per semester. Students who study beyond the normal duration and take less than 9 credits per semester will pay the credit fees.

                                                    





10. I am admitted in or before 2017/18 and a full-time UGC funded student. I have obtained approval of taking less than 9 credits and opted to pay credit fee. When will the debit note be revised?





                                                        Revised debit note will be issued to you in about 2 weeks from approval of application on opted to pay credit fee. You will receive an email notification of new debit note in your connect email account when the debit note is ready.

                                                    





11. I have successfully applied zero-subject enrollment, what should I do next?





                                                        If you have not settled the tuition fee debit note, a revised debit note will be issued within 3 working days from approved zero-subject enrollment. You will receive an email notification of new debit note in your connect email account when the debit note is issued.

If you have settled the tuition fee debit note, a zero-subject enrollment fee of $2,105 will be deducted and the remaining balance of tuition fee will be refunded to you by autopay in general.¬†To speed up the refund process, please provide your bank account number at 'Bank Account Info' facility in¬†Student Account Portal (For Students in Taught Programmes: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/ OR Research Students: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/pages/rologin.jsf). You will receive an email notification of successful input of bank account and another notice of payment by autopay in your connect email account when the refund is made.

                                                    





12. I have applied credit transfer, what should I do next?





                                                        The debit note of credit transfer will be issued to you in about 2 weeks. You will receive an email notification in your connect email account when the debit note is issued.

If you have excess balance of tuition fee, refund will be made by autopay in general. To speed up the refund process, please provide your bank account number at 'Bank Account Info' facility in¬†Student Account Portal (For Students in Taught Programmes: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/ OR Research Students: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/pages/rologin.jsf). You will receive an email notification of successful input of bank account and another notice of payment by autopay in your connect email account when the refund is made.

                                                    





13. I am credit fee paying student and have registered more than 3 credits, why do I only get a debit note for 3 credits?





                                                        According to fee payment schedule for Students in Taught Programmes at FO website, initial fee of 3 credits will be charged for all credit fee paying students.

After add/drop period, debit note for the remaining fee, if any, will be issued. You will receive an email notification for payment of remaining fee when the debit note is issued.

                                                    





14. I have finished my study and will be graduated, what should I do with the outstanding debit note for next semester?





                                                        After your OVERALL examination result is confirmed, your outstanding debit note for next semester shall be cancelled.

                                                    

























Applying NLS / ENLS                                        




15. I am a local government funded students enrolled on a full-time programme, and have borrowed the Non-means-tested Loan (NLS). Do I need to pay tuition fee on my own?





                                                        If your borrowed NLS will be released by the government SFO, you should receive a Remittance Advice which states your NLS disbursement date (drawdown date) to PolyU.

If the NLS disbursement date is¬†ON or BEFORE¬†your tuition fee payment deadline, you should check and make sure that a new debit note is issued with NLS (fully or partially) to offset tuition fee. You will receive an email notice of new debit note after the NLS offset and you should check your debit note details and pay the remaining tuition fee before the deadline, if any.

If the NLS disbursement date is¬†AFTER¬†your tuition fee payment deadline, you should settle your outstanding tuition fee debit note first. After your NLS from SFO reaches PolyU, refund of NLS will be made by autopay to your bank account¬†provided via вАШBank Account InfoвАЩ facility in Student Account Portal (For Students in Taught Programmes: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/ OR Research Students: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/pages/rologin.jsf) within one week of the disbursement date. You will receive an email notice of payment by autopay when the payment is made.

If you have financial difficulties in settling tuition fee and you are waiting for your NLS application result, you may contact SAO for assistance at least 2 days before payment deadline.

                                                    





16. I am a self-financed student and would like to get a payment schedule for applying Non-means-tested Loan (NLS), what should I do?





                                                        You can obtain the annual payment schedule from FO website. You should approach your department to certify the tuition fee of subjects to be registered for applying the Non-means-tested Loan (NLS).¬†


























Refund and Bank Account Information                                        




17. I have dropped subject(s) during add/drop period, how can I get the refund?





                                                        Any excess balance of tuition fee will be refunded to you by autopay in general. In order to speed up the refund process, please provide your bank account number at вАШBank Account InfoвАЩ facility in Student Account Portal (For Students in Taught Programmes: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/ OR Research Students: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/pages/rologin.jsf). Email notification shall be sent to your connect email account to confirm your successful input of bank account and another email notification to advise the payment date when the refund is made.

                                                    





18. I am admitted in or before 2017/18 and a full-time UGC funded student. I have obtained approval of taking less than 9 credits and opted to pay credit fee. How will I get refund of excess tuition fee paid?





                                                        Finance Office will refund excess balance of tuition fee to you by autopay in general. In order to speed up the refund process, please provide your bank account number at вАШBank Account InfoвАЩ facility in Student Account Portal (For Students in Taught Programmes: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/ OR Research Students: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/pages/rologin.jsf). Email notification shall be sent to your connect email account to confirm your successful input of bank account and another email notification to advise the payment date when the refund is made.

                                                    





19. How will Finance Office arrange refund to me?




Tuition fees paid are normally not refundable except in the case of programme cancellation or in the cases mentioned in FAQ 11, 12, 17 & 18 above.
Refund to students will be made by autopay in general. Please open a bank account in Hong Kong if you have not got one and enter your bank account information at вАШBank Account InfoвАЩ facility in Student Account Portal (For Students in Taught Programmes: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/ OR Research Students: https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/pages/rologin.jsf). An email notification to confirm your successful input of bank account shall be sent to your connect email account, whilst another email will to inform you the date of payment when the refund is arranged.

For students under age of 18 and all students including non-local students, they can open a bank account with any bank in Hong Kong. For details, please checking with the bank directly. 







20. Will PolyU deduct any amount from my bank account directly?





                                                        No. The bank account provided by you via 'Bank Account Info' facility in¬†Student Account Portal¬†will only be used for arranging refund or payment from PolyU to you. Finance Office will NOT and CANNOT deduct any amount from your bank account directly.

                                                    





21. I want to submit my bank account information, but the system didn't response?





                                                        FOвАЩs Student Account Portal is best viewed with the latest version of Google Chrome.

                                                    





22. I am an exchange-in student and do not have bank account in Hong Kong. How can I get refund from the University?





                                                        Finance Office will issue an uncrossed cheque to exchange-in student.  Student has to ensure student name in university record completely the same as the name in identity documentation (e.g. passport).  By presenting the uncrossed cheque together with the identity documentation, student can obtain cash refund from any branch of Hang Seng Bank.

                                                    

























Scholarship or Financial Assistance Information                                        




23. Where can I find Scholarship or Financial Assistance information?





                                                        You may visit the following webpages for more information:
Scholarship - http://www.polyu.edu.hk/sao/srss/scholarships/scholarship-types
Financial Assistance - https://www.polyu.edu.hk/sao/srss/financial-assistance/financial-assistance-introduction/
¬†

													Special Support and Counselling

												


Special Support and Counselling












The Student Resources and Support Section (SRSS) of Student Affairs Office will engage newly admitted students with disability or special needs at the beginning of the school term to understand their individual circumstances with a view to offering them with the best possible assistance and support. During their stay at PolyU, SRSS colleagues will keep in touch with them regularly to ensure that their special needs have been well attended to and that they do adjust well to university life.
Counselling service is available to all students at the Counselling and Wellness Section of Student Affairs Office.
Enquiries
2766 6800 or¬†sen.support@polyu.edu.hk¬†

Special Announcement



15 Jul 2026

The PolyU Job Board will be temporarily suspended and unavailable on 21 July 2026 (Tuesday), from 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM due to system maintenance. Services will resume after maintenance is completed. Should you experience any issues afterwards, please feel free to contact the Careers and Placement Section, Student Affairs Office at career.cps@polyu.edu.hk for assistance.

Non-local Student Services



















Non-local Student Services                                                    

The Non-Local Student Services Team (NLSST), Student Affairs Office, provides Mainland and International Students with a wide range of student-centred support services and learning experiences throughout their studies at PolyU towards their development and enrichment.¬† Collaborating with local and non-local students and staff, we aim to strengthen integration among PolyU students.













Contact Us                                                    

Phone: (852) 2766 5408
Email: nls.services@polyu.edu.hk
Website: http://www.polyu.edu.hk/nlss
Address: QT308, 3/F, Chow Yei Ching Building (Entrance at Core T), The Hong Kong Polytechnic University
Opening Hours: 9:00 a.m. - 6:00 p.m. (Monday - Friday)
 12:45 p.m. - 2:00 p.m. (Lunch Break)

Special Announcement



15 Jul 2026

The PolyU Job Board will be temporarily suspended and unavailable on 21 July 2026 (Tuesday), from 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM due to system maintenance. Services will resume after maintenance is completed. Should you experience any issues afterwards, please feel free to contact the Careers and Placement Section, Student Affairs Office at career.cps@polyu.edu.hk for assistance.

Supporting Students with Special Educational Needs













Supporting Students with Special Educational Needs
¬†
PolyU welcomes students with special educational needs (SEN) and is committed to providing an accessible learning environment and inclusive campus atmosphere for all students. Students with SEN, including those with attention deficit/hyperactivity disorder, autism spectrum disorder, hearing impairment, mental illness, physical impairment, speech impairment, visual impairment, visceral disability/chronic illness, and specific learning difficulties, are encouraged to voluntarily declare their needs to the university.
¬†
Admission and Disclosure
PolyU follows a non-discriminatory policy in admitting students with SEN. Students are encouraged to disclose their disabilities and provide appropriate documentation as early as possible to facilitate the arrangement of necessary support.
¬†
Confidentiality
StudentsвАЩ SEN will be kept strictly confidential and will only be disclosed to staff/personnel involved in curriculum provision/coordination on a need-to-know basis. Prior consent will be obtained from the student with SEN if there is a genuine need to disclose his/her SEN to fellow students.
¬†
Accommodation and Support
Student Affairs Office (SAO) provides coordination and support for students with SEN during their studies. Please click here to browse a list of available services for students with SEN. 
The University has a wealth of expertise in rehabilitation to provide support to students with SEN. Experts from various departments, under the coordination of the Student Affairs Office (SAO), could be mobilised to offer assistance to faculties, schools, departments, and individual students as needed.
Academic departments/Schools will provide students with SEN reasonable adjustments to enable them to compete on equal footing with fellow students but without compromising academic standards. Various departments e.g. SAO, host departments, subject offering departments, the Office of Undergraduate Studies, will jointly provide necessary special support and an equitable learning environment to students with SEN.
¬†
Resources for Strengthening Capacity towards SEN Support 
SAO offers workshops, online learning materials and various activities to the PolyU community to facilitate support and communication with students with SEN and to promote inclusion awareness on campus.¬† Staff and students are encouraged to make good of these resources.
¬†
Quality Assurance 
To ensure SEN students have due support and to identify any service gaps promptly, SAO outreaches to these students in every semester, arranges meetings with them every year to collect their feedback and conducts an annual survey to gauge their satisfaction level with SEN support provided by the University. 
The Network on Students with Special Needs (NSSN), chaired by the Dean of Students or his/her delegate and comprised of representatives from about 29 academic and non-academic units of the University, has been established to facilitate the provision and coordination of SEN support.
¬†
 If you have any feedback or questions regarding SEN support, please do not hesitate to contact us via email at sen.support@polyu.edu.hk or by phone at 2766 6800.
¬†

Special Announcement



15 Jul 2026

The PolyU Job Board will be temporarily suspended and unavailable on 21 July 2026 (Tuesday), from 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM due to system maintenance. Services will resume after maintenance is completed. Should you experience any issues afterwards, please feel free to contact the Careers and Placement Section, Student Affairs Office at career.cps@polyu.edu.hk for assistance.

													Counselling and Wellness

												




													Student Counselling

												




													Counselling

												




													Meeting with our Counsellors

												


Meeting with our Counsellors














Appointment Making (POSS)
What is Student Counselling?
Assessment / Test
Emergency Support









We welcome you to meet up with us if you:

Look for personal growth and self-actualization
Want to understand yourself better and willing to make changes
Experience psychological distress
Feel like you need help
Have concern over your mental health or psychological well-being

Confidentiality

The Counsellor has an obligation to respect the confidentiality of information obtained from students in the course of his/her work as a Counsellor except in emergency circumstances in which not to do so would lead to obvious danger to the person or to others or required by law. As and when required, the Counsellor will explain to the service recipients the limits of confidentiality.
All personal information a student provided will be kept solely for the purposes related to the provision of counselling services.

Students in need can make an appointment through the following ways:

Online: Login the POSS system¬†http://www.polyu.edu.hk/poss
Email:¬†stud.counselling@polyu.edu.hk¬†
Phone: (852) 2766 6800 during office hours¬†
Walk-in: Reception counter at QT 308, 3/F, Chow Yei Ching Building (Entrance at Core T)
    Opening Hours
    Monday вАУ Friday: 9.00 am вАУ 6.00 pm (Closed during lunch hour from 12.45 pm to 2.00 pm)
    Saturday, Sunday and Public Holidays: Closed

Students can call¬†PolyU LINE¬†at 8100 1583 during non-office hours.

Non-JUPAS












Overview

The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU) aspires to be an innovative world-class university with a strong sense of social responsibility, driven by its motto, вАЬTo learn and to apply, for the benefit of mankind".








                    How to define local and non-local applicants?
                







                    Could I enroll for more than one programme at the same time?

Admissions


Non-JUPAS Admissions (Senior Year Entry)




                        Frequently Asked Questions
                    

                        Non-JUPAS Senior Year
                    

















6
Frequently Asked Questions




Frequently Asked Questions










General


When is the application period?


Please click here to check the application period. Departments will consider applications on a rolling basis until places are filled. ¬†




How do I get information on schemes / programmes and admission?


Please click the individual scheme / programme entry here to check the scheme / programme information, application deadlines and other details. ¬†




Can I apply through JUPAS as well as to PolyU directly?


If you have obtained HKDSE results as well as other qualifications, either of which alone is sufficient to qualify you for consideration for admission to our schemes / programmes, you can apply either through JUPAS on the basis of HKDSE results or directly to PolyU on the basis of other qualifications. However, you can only choose to apply through one of these two routes for schemes / programmes offered under JUPAS in the same academic year. If you apply through JUPAS and to PolyU concurrently, your direct application to PolyU will NOT be considered. Please however observe UGC's guidelines on inter-institutional transfer of students irrespective of the routes you choose.¬†If you have obtained HKDSE results only, you should apply through JUPAS. Please refer to the JUPAS website for details. ¬†




Can I be considered as a mature applicant?


Those who have reached the age of 25 before 1 September in the year in which they seek admission are categorised as mature applicants automatically for consideration of Departments. Mature applicants without the stipulated entrance qualifications may only be admitted on an individual and exceptional basis, and they must demonstrate sufficient motivation, knowledge and potential to indicate a high probability of being able to complete the scheme / programme successfully. It is not necessary for applicants to apply as a Mature Applicant. We will automatically take this into consideration.




Who should apply for senior year admission?


Associate Degree / Higher Diploma final year students or graduates should apply for senior year admission to our full-time degree schemes / programmes. They should select the schemes / programmes specified for senior year admission via our eAdmission. If you would also like to be considered for admission to the first year of the 4-year degree programmes in case your senior year applications are not successful, please indicate such intention in your application.




What are Top-up degree programmes?


Top-up degree programmes are designated for Associate Degree / Higher Diploma holders. The normal duration of top-up degree programmes is usually 2 years. You may read the details here. Associate Degree / Higher Diploma final year students or graduates looking for articulation opportunities could also apply for admission to these programmes.




Would you consider a non-final year AD/HD student with very outstanding academic results for Senior Year / Top-up Degree admission?


Non-final year AD/HD students with very outstanding academic results should apply for Year 1 admission of a 4-year degree scheme / programme. Depending on the relevancy of previous study and grade attainments, credit transfer up to a maximum of 25% of the scheme / programme requirements may be granted.




Can a non-local applicant with AD/HD qualifications apply for Senior Year / Top-up Degree admission?


No. You should apply for admission to PolyUвАЩs undergraduate schemes / programmes as a non-local student. Please click here for more information.




Which scheme / programme considers first-choice-applicants only?


All programme choices are considered with equal priority. No scheme / programme consider first choice applicants only.




Can I apply for re-admission if I have withdrawn from a programme of study at PolyU in the previous academic year?


Students who have been required to withdraw on grounds of academic failure or have been de-registered, and those who have discontinued their studies without completing the proper procedures for official withdrawal, shall not be considered for re-admission to the same scheme/programme/stream in the following academic year. If you apply to the same programme, you shall be disqualified and your application will not be further processed. Fees paid will not be refunded. You are however still eligible to apply for admission to other programmes.




When will the offer of a scheme / programme marked with "subject to approval" be confirmed?


You can assume that the offer is confirmed unless you are separately notified of its non-approval.




What if the scheme / programme I have applied for is cancelled?


In that case, both the tuition fee and application fee (if you have only one scheme / programme choice) paid will be refunded to you.




Where can I find the scheme / programme interview information?


You can click here for more information.












Entrance Requirements


What are the entrance requirements?


Please click here to read the General Entrance Requirement.




Is there any English or Chinese language requirements for Senior Year / Top-up degree admission?


No, unless it is stipulated by individual schemes / programmes. Please visit the вАЬEntrance RequirementвАЭ section of individual schemes / programmes for more information.




Will you consider my HKDSE results for Senior Year / Top-up degree admission?


Unless it is specified by schemes / programmes, applicantsвАЩ HKDSE results will not be considered. Please visit the вАЬEntrance RequirementвАЭ section of individual schemes / programmes for the details.












Fees and Financial Assistance


How much is the application fee?


A non-refundable fee of HK$500 will be charged for each application.




Am I eligible for the financial assistance scheme?


Full-time local students on government-funded schemes / programmes are eligible to apply for financial assistance from both the Government and PolyU. For details, please visit the Student Affairs OfficeвАЩs webpage.




Is the tuition fee paid refundable?


All fees paid will not be refunded except in the case of scheme / programme cancellation. However, for new students who are admitted to government-funded schemes / programmes and apply for withdrawal of study before the start of a semester, exceptional arrangement will be made to refund the tuition fee paid after deducting the initial fee.












Online Application


How should I submit my application?


Please submit your application online via our eAdmission.




Can I submit more than one application?


For our full-time Bachelor's degree schemes / programmes, you can submit only one application, with up to two choices.




How can I verify my application data?


Please log in to your eAdmission account to verify your application data. If you find any discrepancy between your entry and our record, you should contact us immediately (please quote your application number in all your correspondences with PolyU).¬†As the information you entered in the online application form will form the basis on which your application will be considered, you should make sure that it is complete and accurate. Otherwise, your chance of admission may be affected. Any offer made will also be withdrawn if the decision is based on incomplete and inaccurate application data.




How can I update my application data?


Once you have submitted your application, you are not allowed to change the choice(s) you selected earlier. However, you would still be able to update additional educational details / qualifications and upload relevant supporting documents to the online portal.




How can I know my application result?


You will be notified by email if you are shortlisted for interview/test. If you are given an offer or a conditional offer, you will be informed via email/SMS. You may also log in to your eAdmission account from time to time to check the progress of your application.¬†Offers are normally made on a rolling basis between December and August. If you do not receive any notifications by early September, you can assume that your application is unsuccessful. No separate notification will be sent for unsuccessful cases.




What should I do to accept an offer or a conditional offer?


You should:¬†Indicate your acceptance and settle the initial fee before the "last day for payment" shown in the first debit note of your Notice of Offer;Settle the remaining tuition fee before the "last day for payment" shown in the second debit note of your Notice of Offer; andFor conditional offer, you have to complete the procedures of meeting the conditions stated in the Notice of Offer; andPerform online programme registration according to the date and procedures specified in the Notice of Offer.¬†Otherwise, you will be considered as having given up your study place which will be offered to another applicant.




Will I receive multiple offers, and can I decline one to be reconsidered for another programme choice?


Each applicant is considered simultaneously for all programme choices and may receive multiple offers. There is no priority among the choices, so accepting one offer does not affect the chances of admission to the others.¬†However, please note that you may only accept one offer at a time, and each programme choice can be accepted only once.¬†When you accept a new offer, the initial fee paid will be transferred to the new offer.












Documents Submission


Should I submit documents to academic departments direct?


No, except for materials that cannot be uploaded, e.g. design portfolio.¬†Please upload the documents to your online application and departments will be able to read them together with your application data.¬†You NEED NOT submit more documents than required as non-required documents will not be considered by departments. Please check the вАЬAdditional Documents RequiredвАЭ section of e-prospectus for the details.




What should I do if I want to submit additional/supporting documents after application submission?


You are expected to submit all necessary documents in support of your application (except for documents which are not yet available at the time you submit your application) when you submit your application. However, if you need to submit additional / other supporting documents after submission of application, please scan the additional documents and log into your eAdmission account to upload them.¬†Uploaded documents should be:¬†In black and whiteIn A4 sizeWith low quality high compression (e.g. 300dpi)Less than 5 MB per filePDF or JPG files¬†You should not submit any documents to departments direct except for materials that cannot be uploaded, e.g. design portfolio.




I received a conditional offer. Do I need to submit a certified true copy of my supporting documents for meeting conditions?


No. You are only required to upload a copy of your supporting document (it can be a screen capture, photo or an electronic copy of the official document) for meeting conditions on the specified date shown in the notice of offer via our eAdmission.




When should I submit the original / certified true copy of my supporting documents to PolyU?


After accepting an offer / meeting the conditions of an offer, please arrange documents required for verification to be sent directly by the awarding bodies to the Academic Registry of PolyU at your earliest time (at least 5 to 7 working days before the first day of programme registration). Upon completion of the verification process, we will send you an email reminding you to complete the programme registration online. Please refer to your notice of offer for details.¬†Failure to provide required documents for verification or any discrepancies found in the submitted documents may lead to the cancellation of your offer and registration at the University. Fees paid will not be refunded.




My academic documents are not in English. What should I do?


Please provide officially translated copies of the documents certified by your institutions, Ministry of Education or legal authorities in addition to copies of the original documents. Otherwise, your application may not be considered.












Transfer of study / Credits


Will I be granted credit transfer from my previous studies?


For Associate Degree / Higher Diploma graduates who are admitted to our articulation degree programmes or the senior year of our 4-year full-time undergraduate degree schemes / programmes will follow a reduced curriculum of 60 to 75 credits. Further credit transfer will normally not be possible.




If I am studying at PolyU and wish to transfer to another scheme / programme within PolyU, should I apply via the non-JUPAS route?


It is the UniversityвАЩs policy that new students will not be considered for transfer to another scheme / programme offered in the same mode of study during their first semester of registration.¬†If you are studying a government-funded scheme / programme of PolyU and wish to transfer to another PolyU government-funded scheme / programme of the same level of award, you should submit an application for transfer of study instead of a new application in the non-JUPAS application period.












Non-Academic Achievements


Will my non-academic achievements be considered?


PolyU supports the all-round development of students and gives due recognition to the non-academic achievements of applicants in the admission selection process. Applicants with outstanding non-academic achievements (e.g. with awards/prizes attained in prominent international competitions) will be favourably considered for admission in the selection process.¬†Applicants concerned should provide relevant information and documents in their applications within the application period. We, however, still require applicants to be admitted on the basis of outstanding non-academic achievements to meet the minimum entrance requirements of the schemes / programmes before they are eligible for consideration.




Is it necessary for me to fill the non-academic achievement section of the application form?


No. The non-academic achievement section is optional.




Is there any special arrangement to help sportsmen to manage their studies?


To enable sportsmen to manage their participation in trainings, competitions and academic studies, the normal duration for completing the programmes for students admitted via Outstanding Sportsmen Recommendation Scheme (OSRS) will be automatically extended for two years with retroactive effect from the 2020/21 intake cohort.¬†Eligible OSRS candidates will be considered automatically by the Student-Athlete Learning Support and Admission (SALSA) Scheme. Holistic support services will also be provided to student-athletes throughout their study at PolyU, please refer to here for details.




What is the Special Talents Admission and Recognition Scheme (STARS)?


Students who have demonstrated remarkable talents in sports, arts and culture, leadership and community services, and STEM will be considered under Special Talents Admission and Recognition Scheme (STARS). It aims to serve as an alternative admission route for those whose exceptional achievements cannot be fully reflected in their academic results. STARS applicants to PolyU's undergraduate programmes will be assessed based on the non-academic achievements, self-description and referees submitted in their applications. ¬†Selection panels will assess these applications and will make result announcement from April onwards.












Online Programme Registration


Do I need to come in-person for programme registration?


No, it is not necessary for admittees to perform programme registration in person starting from the 2021/22 academic year. You just need to follow the instructions in your notice of offer to perform programme registration online.

2|vпњљqqxпњљпњљпњљпњљ}xпњљпњљпњљtпњљпњљeD]пњљпњљMAPпњљ№Впњљпњљ]пњљhпњљYпњљпњљs?DпњљQ@MпњљHs	?пњљ@	пњљr\пњљriпњљ*6rпњљ9пњљ>пњљ9пњљзМґ8пњљпњљпњљQпњљ)C^*пњљO6пњљ77:`пњљ’±пњљ|пњљ√†пњљ.пњљпњљM3пњљпњљFпњљk>Cпњљ)sпњљпњљпњљrпњљfпњљпњљjhпњљпњљпњљпњљTпњљ$&пњљEYпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљHпњљпњљпњљdпњљпњљUZtпњљhLпњљiпњљ␀’®x%пњљўЬпњљпњљHпњљпњљ«Шпњљпњљyпњљпњљпњљ(}пњљi_YD6пњљjpёСпњљpwпњљпњљпњљ?pпњљ}3пњљYпњљпњљvЁАwпњљcпњљ,[пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ\vпњљ!пњљпњљcпњљUпњљпњљ<пњљпњљпњљпњљOпњљ7s»Ы-пњљ@ќ±?^'пњљ?Q45пњљпњљпњљG0cRпњљпњљtпњљёљпњљiпњљ0eпњљпњљпњљ<пњљ5Qt,пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљiP%i2пњљпњљпњљiпњљ␀"пњљ *cj\пњљRпњљZ1пњљ5&QпњљTпњљkxпњљпњљaпњљпњљlпњљпњљK{пњљ÷Д?пњљ08tпњљпњљqпњљ;пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ\пњљhпњљпњљпњљ:пњљMLпњљCi9X'пњљпњљпњљпњљ

X0пњљпњљh)36)+пњљcпњљQ∆Їnпњљпњљ'?пњљ-пњљпњљпњљпњљ<пњљC%тПЯЙAпњљпњљCпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ8пњљпњљ0)q/ЋµпњљпњљпњљпњљaпњљBR/пњљпњљ(iпњљпњљ}пњљпњљ}пњљc-пњљpпњљxпњљP);z0)пњљпњљпњљIY1uпњљ>rpпњљ_пњљZпњљпњљ4*пњљпњљ»БJ\пњљпњљ1пњљ#пњљ/пњљпњљпњљD1ЁМпњљ…ґпњљй™±пњљ<|пњљпњљQ(пњљпњљEVпњљпњљпњљQwпњљp}пњљ@'пњљ
KPпњљEwDпњљH6пњљZпњљпњљпњљ*пњљT!пњљпњљ8Iпњљ7G5пњљeпњљPпњљпњљ 1пњљпњљ<пњљ*`пњљyпњљпњљX6tпњљd<пњљQпњљпњљtBпњљпњљn(.пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљsпњљпњљ$пњљпњљfTG6пњљпњљпњљпњљ!пњљHUпњљпњљпњљ␀@пњљпњљJпњљпњљT$Xпњљпњљqпњљ

пњљLпњљпњљ!g]пњљпњљпњљпњљ?sпњљYпњљпњљi6пњљNпњљпњљWпњљACпњљ:пњљ-пњљ{<пњљќ°пњљпњљSпњљпњљO^пњљ^пњљќњ-AпњљпњљI8пњљ␀пњљ8SпњљcOпњљ*пњљпњљ(пњљпњљDпњљQпњљiпњљZпњљпњљ]пњљпњљzпњљ\~пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ÷Ппњљ;пњљG~пњљпњљ5,

f
пњљZпњљ"@пњљ_пњљ*-пњљ#Vпњљпњљпњљ/A,пњљЌґпњљY-xпњљџГ;пњљvпњљпњљЋґc}Uпњљпњљ;пњљ^GпњљпњљL\пњљпњљпњљпњљ\яЇ∆Г\fпњљ~пњљLпњљяІпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљg''En)? СпњљlпњљJzпњљпњљs0пњљrпњљXGпњљпњљvпњљ#;пњљnпњљпњљпњљ»Ъпњљd[пњљ~+.пњљпњљdr1пњљпњљjпњљЋВYпњљпњљ—°пњљпњљ¬Кiпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљnпњљ,cXiйПХпњљyпњљFёЪпњљ]№Јпњљпњљпњљ~Sпњљ>пњљ~пњљ+пњљco7Yпњљё£bпњљпњљпњљпњљ1zпњљ@7пњљпњљrпњљпњљпњљ&пњљgпњљпњљGпњљ,+пњљпњљпњљTj—І,пњљпњљ.пњљ␀R"9пњљbпњљ*пњљNgпњљ@пњљkпњљKпњљ?xпњљUпњљпњљ|XЁДx$dbпњљ`пњљпњљS7пњљ|пњљIпњљ0wпњљ1␀пњљпњљ)пњљпњљBпњљvѕ§пњљпњљпњљv!M.uпњљпњљxпњљпњљCYпњљv!)a}пњљ:MfпњљпњљTiпњљпњљkпњљxпњљM8

пњљпњљL;&uпњљA	пњљ.Mпњљпњљпњљfпњљћ≠ЌЕпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ~пњљпњљJ_пњљпњљOпњљf3j.џЖпњљпњљ.пњљrпњљпњљ`«іпњљ"^,ѕ°пњљпњљC'к£Дпњљпњљ ИоИ©!Yпњљпњљ␀пњљпњљпњљmпњљ,пњљDпњљ<␀пњљпњљLеҐЧпњљE1№Фпњљfпњљe"пњљpпњљHпњљOпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ∆АпњљnX<пњљ[пњљAnH~rYnпњљ>:пњљ>пњљпњљпњљeпњљ3t3пњљпњљпњљпњљ	пњљl(^xпњљ\пњљпњљпњљпњљR

пњљпњљ пњљ(пњљD[P)пњљпњљTjпњљ>XEnпњљпњљd&÷УKпњљ,пњљ!пњљпњљSпњљёЃ8пњљпњљPiпњљ8mK;пњљiпњљNSпњљпњљT>пњљпњљпњљ#пњљ:Nпњљ9пњљ|пњљ"пњљпњљEp-пњљK/CпњљTпњљпњљпњљs8пњљпњљP~	wпњљqпњљnпњљпњљdIT…§пњљ—Љпњљпњљ—Љ\}aпњљпњљ3u<пњљпњљk√ЯD?] пњљtЌГпњљ_oJпњљ&пњљпњљIfпњљ,пњљпњљпњљ,пњљ,пњљЏІPVпњљпњљnпњљCпњљe}пњљпњљ2c,U`пњљпњљпњљcJ пњљпњљjпњљпњљпњљPпњљ	6пњљTпњљпњљпњљt,G[%пњљXV;пњљ пњљпњљпњљпњљ1пњљ^_пњљw~пњљWпњљпњљпњљ7пњљпњљ9пњљ|‘ЗOnw$;пњљпњљOпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ}пњљпњљ#пњљпњљпњљ$пњљпњљ-?пњљпњљпњљпњљLпњљпњљDпњљпњљHпњљпњљпњљr>пњљrd7yпњљпњљdZu~TпњљпњљVпњљx/÷НпњљTYz7пњљtпњљrпњљW@пњљ*7EUпњљпњљпњљ0,!Jпњљ“Спњљпњљnпњљ:пњљ#√Ј{qпњљпњљпњљ«Щпњљ-пњљ÷µVпњљ-пњљ„¶:lпњљP ;nпњљZ3}пњљf* пњљZ3пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ9пњљzCпњљ29пњљ␀9rпњљvAпњљYпњљпњљ*cupoпњљпњљпњљKпњљa&K^пњљ+Xпњљ%пњљ/ЎЗIпњљEn
пњљZlпњљпњљпњљ√УпњљI09ZпњљпњљпњљdпњљпњљiI"ZпњљпњљпњљIhпњљqпњљпњљqKпњљпњљqпњљпњљпњљпњљ`mU1яЛпњљ*пњљ5пњљUпњљYпњљпњљ$*пњљпњљDпњљ] пњљyпњљпњљ&1пњљпњљпњљпњљbXпњљпњљпњљпњљзМЪпњљпњљпњљsпњљNпњљ8пњљпњљ~nV&Q`пњљDпњљ‘Є пњљ*'[пњљпњљЌ£пњљTпњљпњљпњљпњљbWпњљ’Кпњљпњљ\l}
T№†пњљuпњљyU:пњљпњљJпњљпњљпњљпњљ≈¶Tпњљ:пњљbOпњљпњљпњљпњљ<пњљпњљaпњљ2пњљпњљ}пњљпњљпњљl/Oпњљпњљпњљ-qVпњљnпњљ.X;пњљUKпњљRпњљ;пњљ]3пњљƒЩпњљпњљAпњљпњљпњљ}PпњљEwAпњљv1пњљяВпњљпњљпњљzпњљMпњљkпњљпњљsпњљпњљCEпњљs;N^пњљпњљMпњљпњљпњљ £м°Л6 IпњљQпњљ|пњљпњљPDwпњљпњљXQпњљm@%ў®QпњљпњљпњљMпњљпњљdпњљпњљпњљtпњљпњљЎГпњљ¬љPпњљпњљ{пњљпњљo.пњљyпњљпњљgпњљпњљ.пњљпњљеЗУzпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљQeпњљпњљ|#пњљпњљoпњљQ?пњљпњљпњљ.8zпњљXпњљпњљ3пњљX/=sпњљпњљпњљhHeзЪїkпњљпњљпњљпњљlпњљpпњљпњљpпњљ

xпњљпњљпњљпњљkпњљ0пњљпњљпњљ?пњљ]пњљпњљпњљпњљ5пњљпњљIlPпњљпњљпњљпњљ>пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљw'џ±cпњљ’ђ# пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ5;99пњљпњљпњљ>c"F69пњљпњљyпњљ=Wпњљy!пњљпњљпњљHпњљ0пњљпњљпњљl1ѕ≥пњљGпњљ[пњљ_пњљXпњљпњљ3пњљ$<пњљпњљeпњљTvпњљt%Wпњљtпњљ%]пњљTпњљпњљпњљ]пњљпњљпњљ)c=пњљrl[rKџТK’≥=пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ>!пњљO2	пњљ;Rпњљпњљ1пњљ{Hпњљ!пњљ
vпњљgпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ{Yпњљв°ђ\пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљc	≈ЬпњљJW|y"пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ…≥пњљпњљzhпњљ2пњљпњљX.пњљzпњљ)пњљпњљпњљпњљ]пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ>P)4пњљЋЊпњљпњљ%≈Љпњљ№©пњљпњљotпњљ|Aпњљwпњљhпњљм≤ђпњљ>пњљпњљ*пњљ3a,пњљ
пњљSпњљ␀пњљvs[пњљпњљxKVпњљ>1AпњљвЊђпњљпњљпњљпњљJпњљ/пњљпњљпњљпњљ]пњљpY Дпњљ␀пњљџЧпњљпњљпњљпњљ-пњљпњљпњљbпњљrmпњљ>пњљпњљ>zKY.пњљwпњљпњљпњљ@s=пњљ␀пњљ<␀mпњљ	пњљ7]пњљhпњљпњљ0hпњљ»©пњљпњљ=!пњљ@пњљPпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ
dпњљпњљ&лґНпњљdn пњљR#VпњљKпњљпњљ.пњљпњљaпњљ}wпњљпњљпњљ2пњљ=пњљпњљ␀пњљ+$пњљiпњљ:пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљaN:пњљQпњљ<пњљпњљS0пњљ/5N Р.пњљkпњљпњљuпњљпњљB+пњљkпњљпњљпњљ&пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ4jz:NпњљFпњљпњљIпњљпњљx+0E)пњљпњљпњљпњљRпњљgпњљZпњљпњљH{zџИпњљTпњљnDпњљK;пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ%пњљпњљyпњљp—Љkhi'пњљ:пњљMjпњљпњљDпњљnfпњљпњљvjпњљn>

xпњљпњљ]]пњљпњљпњљ}пњљпњљпњљпњљ`пњљпњљ$Xпњљ	vaпњљ0,пњљ`пњљalOl!пњљ∆Рe пњљпњљ{oпњљ<пњљYEMcпњљбЭєdпњљпњљNпњљпњљTпњљпњљyпњљ~пњљpпњљvпњљ3пњљпњљпњљ#пњљ#пњљzпњљpƒЖпњљ}ƒПпњљoпњљGпњљ/пњљпњљпњљ#пњљrпњљqпњљпњљ_пњљпњљпњљC;jmG9пњљпњљпњљпњљZ9Jпњљпњљtпњљпњљ_R,пњљпњљ!пњљCvпњљпњљbпњљ?пњљyпњљW!E|пњљпњљпњљпњљ9!c–К~yпњљsпњљпњљпњљ3пњљs=пњљгіРпњљпњљq:5пњљпњљc80Tпњљ-
пњљGпњљ~?9^;пњљ	`~1пњљпњљh4␀пњљiпњљпњљпњљDпњљпњљ#пњљпњљпњљ∆ДпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљrпњљGпњљ)aпњљG,g>zпњљOLпњљпњљпњљ;пњљ^пњљ\Gпњљпњљ;пњљoпњљпњљaўЮпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ;пњљ4
L}пњљ+rbпњљ6пњљ1пњљHЋДyпњљ/пњљEdпњљn3пњљ;пњљIпњљпњљпњљпњљEDпњљ8^пњљпњљ'пњљпњљaпњљKchqпњљпњљ%пњљпњљпњљ9пњљAпњљќАKпњљпњљпњљ	N m-пњљIпњљпњљпњљ{пњљ^!пњљ2пњљ}9sпњљ;Zпњљ-пњљпњљAЌЩ-~/3пњљ24пњљ\пњљ";пњљпњљпњљRпњљпњљпњљпњљlпњљпњљпњљпњљd]`<пњљпњљJolQ.пњљпњљ-пњљпњљKPпњљIпњљпњљпњљD?пњљD'пњљпњљпњљпњљ>пњљпњљпњљL$HпњљX`пњљпњљzFyпњљeЌШyпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљZпњљпњљћЄпњљпњљLпњљпњљp}пњљ(пњљgпњљQ;пњљпњљиЧИ2пњљKяѓпњљпњљD?oпњљпњљD?o'pпњљпњљпњљ№Фпњљпњљпњљ=пњљ^пњљ7ЌМaпњљDпњљo	пњљKiпњљќЬ~пњљfпњљI?GпњљпњљIпњљ0пњљ!G@)пњљAпњљпњљ8sпњљ~kLпњљпњљпњљпњљ0пњљпњљ3.пњљZпњљ-пњљпњљh"G пњљ5t8sпњљ~'-пњљ2пњљHќЬ~пњљ9пњљD?Gпњљa+2пњљ@пњљ4#NfпњљVfXпњљпњљпњљпњљAпњљVfdпњљпњљпњљпњљa;’Щпњљ2пњљпњљпњљпњљ^пњљпњљ1пњљ

!on␀(пњљsпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ3пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ$#Xfпњљdпњљc>Jпњљ9пњљl$rпњљIпњљNпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ,пњљDHж©ЭQ,Wпњљпњљпњљ&пњљпњљgiпњљP)пњљrпњљLпњљпњљ6пњљUпњљпњљ8sпњљпњљ…Ь?пњљmпњљпњљўҐ№СпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљYпњљN&пњљпњљпњљ,пњљdпњљ8sXƒЯпњљяЙl

ё≠LпњљпњљL*eпњљпњљRпњљпњљb*пњљyпњљпњљ[пњљPпњљ\пњљпњљq-пњљпњљs+пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљмєХwuпњљ/пњљV3пњљzk%|LпњљyпњљпњљпњљпњљPпњљ\пњљпњљOпњљмєХgsпњљ_пњљпњљkпњљпњљ їпњљпњљsпњљTпњљпњљпњљ>пњљпњљ<\7;пњљjпњљJ~y:пњљпњљs+пњљпњљпњљDпњљпњљмєУgWпњљw+S%'пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљTYпњљpпњљмСЫпњљ+пњљпњљпњљfѕ≠<пњљпњљпњљuYпњљпњљVпњљпњљ@пњљпњљL

RпњљIvпњљпњљ-PпњљпњљMпњљ”ҐпњљZпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљP'пњљпњљ+пњљпњљ ’≠Dпњљ%пњљZx4Hu+—ОzпњљпњљпњљIпњљZ4пњљпњљпњљeпњљпњљпњљпњљ’Љ~¬Тпњљпњљпњљx2Hu+пњљfпњљпњљƒУAпњљ[пњљvпњљпњљ.пњљ-{Sпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљiпњљ5?пњљпњљпњљ^пњљпњљFWпњљпњљпњљiMпњљ{пњљ6K$пњљ2пњљ÷§пњљпњљм®ЈY 1пњљпњљB4пњљпњљ—µпњљпњљпњљпњљ8o)oпњљ+пњљпњљпњљ&’љDпњљ%пњљ_ OkRпњљKпњљ\Gпњљпњљ)TпњљпњљпњљG„ЪпњљSпњљwпњљ)пњљпњљJ~пњљ<пњљIu/пњљa	пњљNпњљ„§пњљпњљxпњљпњљ FWSпњљпњљпњљпњљ.пњљпњљSпњљwпњљ)пњљпњљJ~пњљ<пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ ’≠пњљЋХ1пњљпњљB4пњљпњљпњљeпњљsпњљwпњљ)пњљпњљJ~пњљ<пњљпњљпњљи∞Д_5пњљпњљn%^пњљ$пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљZпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљЌЕ)пњљпњљJ~пњљ<пњљпњљпњљи∞Д_;пњљпњљn%^пњљ$пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљmпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљeпњљпњљпњљT~пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљѕУ_>/пњљn%^пњљ$пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ<пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљSqr*пњљ~^RпњљJtX¬ѓпњљпњљTwпњљпњљпњљnпњљпњљdoRпњљNќХ)пњљпњљпњљ2пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљTпњљпњљ ѓпњљпњљTпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ ’≠пњљЋХ1пњљпњљB4пњљпњљпњљeпњљsпњљм≤ЫпњљSпњљпњљпњљjпњљпњљVпњљпњљ~пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљxпњљпњљ FWSпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљMпњљпњљпњљfпњљЁЬпњљ ѓпњљWпњљTпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ ’ЭDпњљ~пњљ[пњљ&ўЫTпњљSwпњљпњљ}tпњљпњљпњљпњљo)пњљпњљT~пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљи∞Д_?пњљпњљn%^пњљ$tпњљё§пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљe'пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ ’≠Dпњљ%пњљпњљy3Hu+пњљz%aHпњљ&Uпњљ4<пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљcпњљпњљNNпњљпњљѕЫAпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ4пњљ_?oпњљn%^пњљ$пњљё§пњљпњљOпњљ-O]k~ќІпњљпњљќІпњљ.пњљ~пњљ÷§пњљпњљпњљпњљT~пњљпњљпњљIu/пњљr%AпњљпњљпњљPпњљC]k~пњљпњљпњљeпњљпњљЋѓпњљпњљ5пњљпњљ%:9пњљ_?okRпњљKпњљ\Iпњљпњљ)TAC
]}пњљпњљ9пњљпњљпњљtпњљпњљпњљn\пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљѕ≥_?пњљkRпњљKпњљ\Iпњљпњљ)TACyпњљZBпњљ.пњљпњљпњљstпњљпњљпњљѕїAпњљ[пњљKпњљпњљпњљnпњљпњљNпњљS?пњљ-пњљпњљпњљj

5пњљJпњљtпњљпњљTxпњљ7пњљпњљпњљ1пњљOпњљпњљпњљпњљzQ`tпњљ1пњљпњљl№≠пњљпњљnzпњљ”Ы^~пњљпњљ-пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљEпњљпњљ1пњљпњљпњљyMпњљGпњљпњљпњљ-OпњљпњљM

пњљ^Oпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ7пњљпњљпњљпњљy|пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљIпњљпњљпњљпњљvпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ8пњљпњљпњљпњљ„ѓпњљпњљпњљЋЗпњљIпњљпњљпњљzпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ+пњљпњљoпњљпњљпњљK|пњљпњљпњљяљxпњљпњљпњљпњљ?пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљfmпњљпњљпњљ„ѓпњљ>пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ#№њпњљпњљV>пњљ{пњљпњљпњљhпњљO&пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљNOпњљJпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ|пњљпњљOпњљпњљпњљпњљ_пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ_пњљxпњљкЗЗ'яљпњљпњљпњљ~|wпњљпњљпњљoпњљ~пњљпњљЁГпњљпњљпњљ_пњљпњљпњљ-,пњљпњљпњљпњљhпњљ5yjпњљпњљпњљ<пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљoxgпњљпњљпњљgпњљ)пњљK_пњљпњљKпњљX*пњљrZ ХRпњљпњљ#пњљR"пњљ

xпњљ}Rпњљnпњљ0пњљпњљ>пњљпњљпњљh$пњљпњљпњљFпњљ–ЗJпњљпњљ^RпњљbпњљCпњљ}пњљn^MпњљX4пњљ3;пњљ<~^пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ*a`ucпњљпњљ}wпњљ

xпњљпњљ}|пњљ≈µпњљпњљпњљпњљoI6ўЉпњљпњљl6!ўДпњљ@xпњљH6O пњљ␀Y*fпњљпњљ%пњљ`@≈Ґпњљ*пњљ.*hпњљпњљпњљlEпњљhпњљe#пњљпњљпњљDoпњљUџВmпњљ’ґпњљпњљЏЦпњљпњљпњљпњљWпњљпњљпњљпњљџАpпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљѕІпњљ~пњљgпњљћЩ9пњљпњљћЩ3пњљ%	ƒИ(пњљBпњљпњљпњљUdпњљпњљпњљBпњљVпњљпњљ^пњљпњљпњљпњљ„Йпњљпњљv_пњљпњљ1пњљпњљпњљ	Qпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ÷ѓпњљпњљZпњљпњљzпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљmYпњљпњљ|пњљпњљпњљ}пњљпњљ’љпњљ=пњљпњљ)|uпњљ
8u5пњљпњљ3nBпњљпњљпњљпњљ^пњљпњљпњљfSпњљпњљATпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљwSпњљkDпњљпњљ[пњљycпњљпњљпњљEпњљпњљ@я±пњљwSпњљЎІvпњљsпњљпњљoCпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ[;пњљhbUпњљпњљпњљH>пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ]oпњљpпњљ}пњљпњљпњљ%}пњљd_пњљ∆ѓ<пњљпњљfGRпњљLпњљ&пњљпњљпњљпњљ∆љ(йПЈ>пњљпњљ—¶wYпњљ1-B÷ђпњљK␀5:Giпњљпњљ>пњљпњљпњљпњљXпњљпњљ$пњљпњљ.пњљIпњљпњљпњљJпњљ$пњљпњљzпњљпњљDпњљhпњљC*D%пњљE*пњљпњљѕ©пњљQпњљ;Jпњљ>ZпњљSпњљ єAпњљпњљ Еrпњљ&FFпњљпњљOпњљ␀пњљпњљпњљ;Pпњљпњљ#EпњљkпњљпњљM6:пњљs^bпњљHпњљпњљWпњљeO)пњљ0пњљпњљJm~пњљпњљ”Ґпњљпњљ_nпњљaпњљ(*kNпњљ»Зпњљпњљ[Aпњљпњљ3H(Dпњљgпњљ%пњљVпњљпњљl6пњљAWпњљпњљпњљпњљdUпњљRpпњљ.пњљ=*V»ЙпњљпњљG{пњљпњљпњљпњљ +2пњљпњљпњљЋСпњљпњљzyпњљl)пњљ

пњљпњљhs!пњљW-пњљпњљZпњљ!пњљпњљпњљUпњљM2пњљ	пњљFпњљпњљпњљ,bпњљ@пњљ>пњљпњљhпњљjпњљпњљпњљ]пњљ1пњљпњљ~пњљпњљяҐ«Цпњљпњљпњљпњљ␀e3пњљпњљпњљ\пњљ#Wпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ 1пњљAbпњљM`"Gпњљпњљ6пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ пњљL`9jZ)пњљoпњљ 1пњљAbпњљпњљ 1пњљAbпњљпњљ 1пњљAbпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљmCbпњљ
пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ 1пњљAпњљпњљпњљxпњљпњљпњљiпњљxпњљпњљƒ≠T!пњљCпњљ"HSпњљпњљLпњљпњљQпњљxпњљпњљпњљяЩRfпњљ*пњљпњљпњљя≠aZ пњљ
eпњљя≠B>пњљя≠пњљVё†"пњљпњљпњљTtпњљ%пњљпњљƒЂпњљпњљћ¶пњљ £пњљ#пњљJ—БпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљTпњљпњљmпњљBпњљ:пњљ9пњљпњљQJUiпњљ8@qпњљll*Sn'пњљпњљHqпњљVпњљQпњљпњљ\%%пњљпњљ␀}пњљ-пњљK)пњљпњљпњљL␀пњљ:пњљпњљ1пњљпњљoпњљпњљпњљпњљm2FG11пњљAbпњљпњљпњљ␀1пњљпњљcQb9пњљпњљпњљпњљ~пњљ|9пњљJпњљ<*пњљпњљпњљпњљRпњљпњљ2пњљпњљпњљ-пњљпњљпњљbпњљпњљ[пњљпњљпњљHuпњљ9пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљHпњљ%hпњљP-пњљh1-EпњљNпњљпњљпњљпњљ+e(пњљпњљпњљWB7¬£пњљпњљпњљпњљoпњљzпњљпњљNпњљпњљпњљcпњљ_Eпњљ_пњљ
пњљпњљпњљ/lпњљc4bпњљпњљrпњљ>пњљпњљqпњљпњљпњљпњљBQIпњљ!пњљпњљпњљ$krJjZzFfVvпњљ-w\T'пњљ]P8пњљпњљпњљSBпњљпњљ'ULпњљ ©пњљhF’•пњљпњљohlпњљ=gпњљ<_3-XЎ≤hq+-YпњљпњљЁњпњљWпњљпњљcпњљ?пњљпњљпњљKyпњљgOпњљпњљпњљEпњљ7dпњљv4пњљ/0пњљпњљeпњљ7

\sпњљ ЉпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљV”Х:пњљ#пњљпњљ9пњљпњљпњљBsпњљпњљпњљF5пњљпњљ4пњљпњљQM’•%пњљFпњљCпњљVпњљпњљfпњљпњљ#}wпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљzzпњљпњљпњљ пњљtпњљпњљпњљ1kuпњљCcGпњљпњљtпњљпњљ`cпњљпњљ

XпњљпњљBпњљiпњљпњљMпњљпњљ*(пњљпњљ~пњљпњљпњљпњљrпњљVпњљпњљпњљ<{Dпњљпњљb*пњљпњљ5пњљпњљпњљxпњљпњљ>MtsпњљN*пњљ{hkdпњљпњљ`яЯпњљпњљпњљпњљTпњљпњљ[f¬ґm8/2xпњљпњљпњљпњљ'пњљпњљпњљbпњљпњљпњљ_пњљ

Tпњљпњљпњљfпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљfпњљ^[CuGv_пњљпњљпњљпњљ{пњљeпњљ>пњљ}2пњљ7пњљqпњљпњљпњљ|(7пњљl[пњљпњљYпњљпњљпњљg>\!?пњљY/,hпњљmпњљпњљ|пњљgпњљпњљпњљ\[пњљ3пњљ35g>пњљiJпњљпњљ#qпњљпњљfпњљпњљпњљпњљ`Zc,iпњљ

3пњљпњљпњљёУпњљпњљпњљ>_zпњљпњљ–Ѓ~пњљeпњљпњљ]Kпњљпњљпњљпњљ{nЌ®Sпњљ,пњљпњљXпњљSпњљxM]euпњљ94пњљ]оХЗe»Ъпњљпњљ`9пњљ,пњљvxlпњљ Ђпњљпњљ#cпњљ#пњљпњљ"пњљ	пњљпњљпњљпњљi(пњљH@P'пњљ&ƒАпњљпњљKпњљпњљ9пњљпњљb5k>#nwпњљпњљ"+zпњљGQпњљ[пњљTпњљпњљпњљпњљ	iпњљпњљпњљAпњљпњљпњљ#gпњљпњљeпњљпњљ6e␀пњљ8пњљпњљG_A0пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљZ.пњљџ©жІҐпњљeпњљbпњљпњљ4пњљпњљ:пњљпњљ5)пњљb1дЯ∞пњљпњљпњљпњљP пњљпњљпњљ*пњљпњљ?пњљпњљпњљ␀пњљEпњљпњљ-пњљlпњљwЋЊ)9Y√Нпњљ4bпњљпњљпњљпњљ9пњљ~Nпњљпњљпњљaпњљ„Єпњљ+пњљUпњљUпњљпњљпњљпњљ0пњљпњљпњљ{пњљпњљ[пњљ-пњљ[пњљpпњљkпњљkпњљпњљ	пњљпњљGпњљ8=.пњљпњљ/sпњљпњљ(пњљ9пњљlпњљ6пњљпњљ'{r{=&пњљпњљпњљxпњљN/пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ8пњљ/пњљAsпњљпњљпњљ(пњљпњљfпњљtпњљHB8пњљeпњљxIпњљпњљпњљ(¬їпњљпњљ‘њпњљb_l_iпњљlgпњљEпњљivr<пњљBZпњљ;ƒіпњљzC(пњљy
~пњљпњљпњљ5Ќ±пњљDпњљпњљпњљ,zпњљпњљпњљпњљxпњљ	пњљ?пњљпњљ'пњљпњљпњљv9vQпњљ8пњљпњљh['kпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ|VxU@пњљпњљsaпњљ
“ФTпњљпњљT~пњљпњљ&пњљпњљ|пњљBKcпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ9"xпњљp.пњљпњљUпњљпњљR пњљпњљ$пњљ$пњљпњљA_пњљпњљ>@p}пњљпњљ?5@9пњљsпњљпњљ1пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљMdпњљX|v.пњљпњљпњљяїпњљпњљxпњљпњљпњљ}пњљ\пњљпњљпњљSпњљпњљ¬ВY^пњљ+пњљпњљпњљiпњљпњљпњљ?пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљ∆Япњљ>пњљ$Mпњљпњљ<пњљпњљd>пњљ~-пњљLпњљпњљ

пњљпњљTпњљпњљ4√≥dпњљ$пњљпњљпњљF ҐnпњљпњљпњљпњљHQпњљ%fkпњљпњљЋЦпњљTпњљпњљпњљпњљbпњљx$4пњљKпњљ_$I*пњљG!c␀gпњљV№ДпњљпњљпњљTHLJ#пњљrпњљпњљпњљ/ge.fK␀'пњљ}пњљпњљ[\пњљ&пњљпњљпњљпњљ4qLпњљ!ћКпњљЎ≥lPJпњљпњљ-пњљпњљx`уОЧЧm|пњљkпњљпњљ/<пњљпњљЏ¶пњљпњљ|пњљ6/пњљпњљ7_пњљпњљпњљ'oпњљпњљпњљ;пњљoпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљяМпњљlпњљbпњљ_пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљUпњљпњљ}пњљпњљ	пњљпњљ9пњљyпњљ

"qпњљпњљпњљ(I0K$пњљпњљпњљпњљпњљпњљPAEпњљпњљ8s№©пњљ9пњљпњљпњљgi*:пњљпњљ␀≈ИпњљпњљпњљB1b	пњљ#~:пњљпњљпњљпњљ/tpпњљA#пњљпњљпњљ4пњљƒЄпњљ{пњљyXпњљпњљ~pпњљпњљp1:пњљcpпњљC пњљ)E3UTпњљuпњљ8ѕїпњљпњљпњљgпњљxпњљпњљ!пњљ#пњљ[пњљпњљpпњљељљпњљпњљ␀пњљD
